details: Table 1 in paper PMC9790764 (DOI 10.1134/S107042802211001X) lists compound 4c (1,4-diaminobutane derivative) with M 526.81, mp 143.5 C; compound name and value match.
Paper PMC10671214 reports compound 6a (Methyl rac-...-2',3''-indoline]-4'-carboxylate) as white powder, mp 255-257 C, matching the row.
Paper PMC10671214 reports compound 5d (Methyl rac-...-5''-bromo-1'-ethyl-...) as white powder, mp >300 C, matching the row.
Paper (DOI 10.1055/s-0037-1609435) gives Octylphosphonic Acid (2f) as a white solid with mp 100-102 C, matching the row.

Paper PMC6193241 lists compound 7g (tert-Butyl 3-{1-[(Pivaloyloxy)(methyl)amino]-2-nitroethyl}-6-chloro-1H-indole-1-carboxylate) with mp 108-109 C; value matches although the supplied evidence_quote actually quotes the preceding 7b block (which has the same mp).
Paper PMC4058664 reports 4-Benzyl-2-mercaptomethyl-2H-phthalazin-1-one (5) recrystallized from ethanol, white crystals, mp 145-146 C.
Paper PMC4058664 reports (4-Benzyl-1-oxo-1H-phthalazin-2-yl)acetic acid hydrazide (7) as white crystals, mp 130-132 C.
Paper PMC4058664 reports Compound 10b ((4-Benzylphthalazin-1-ylamino)phenol) as yellow crystals with mp 236-237 C; name and value match.

Row claims compound 11 is the biphenyl-4,4'-diamine derivative, but in the paper compound 11 is N,N'-bis(4-benzylphthalazin-1-yl)-1,4-phenylenediamine (MW 544, C36H28N6); the biphenyl-4,4'-diamine analog is compound 12 (MW 620, C42H32N6, mp >340 C). The value 250-251 C belongs to compound 11 (phenylenediamine), not the biphenyl-4,4'-diamine named in the row.

Row names compound as 5-(2-Chlorobenzylidene)- variant (V1 in paper, mp 292-293 C), but the value 148-150 C is for the 5-(3-Chlorobenzylidene)- variant (V2). The evidence quote correctly shows V2 data, so the mp belongs to the 3-chloro analogue, not the 2-chloro compound named in the row.
Paper PMC6259447 reports Methyl 2-benzamido-2-(phenylamino)acetate (2), m.p. 124-126 C; compound name and value match.
Paper PMC6259131 reports 1-(5-Benzyl-4-phenyl-4H-[1,2,4]triazol-3-thiomethyl)-3-(4-nitrophenyl)-urea (12b) as yellow crystals, mp 127 C; matches.
Paper PMC6146956 reports 2-(4-Chlorocarbonylphenyl)-5-(6-cyanobenzothiazol-2-yl)furan (4a) with m.p. >300 C; matches.
Paper PMC6146956 reports 2-[4-(6-Cyanobenzothiazol-2-yl)phenyl]-5-(6-cyanobenzothiazol-2-yl)thiophene (6b) with m.p. >300 C; matches.
Paper PMC6264237 gives compound 4a, (1R)-N-(1-(N-tert-butylcarbamoyl)-1-(4-(dimethylamino)pyridyn-3-yl)methyl)-L-valine methyl ester, with mp 119 C for the major diastereomer; matches.

Paper PMC6264237 gives compound 4l, (1SR)-N-(1-(N-tert-Butylcarbamoyl)-1-(4-(dimethylamino)pyridyn-3-yl)methyl) O-benzyl L-serine methyl ester, with mp 145 C for mixture of two diastereomers; matches.
Paper PMC6146789 reports 2-(bromomethyl)benzothiazole (compound 1) as a sticky white powder with m.p. 82 C (decomp.); property=decomposition is correct.
Paper PMC6146942 Table 1 lists compound 2c (X=Cl, R=4-Cl; 6-chloro-3-(4-chlorophenyl)-2-methylquinazoline-4(3H)-thione) with mp 202-204 C.
Paper PMC6236391 reports 2-Deoxy-2-(furfural)-imino-1,3,4,6-tetra-O-acetyl-beta-D-glucopyranoside (8) with m p 114-116 C.
Paper PMC8697427 Table cites Umifenovir Tm = 415 K from reference [58] (Kons et al.); 415-273.15 = 141.85 C unit conversion is correct.
Paper PMC6146921 Table 8 (experimental column) lists 2-methyl-2-propanol with experimental BP 82.40 C.
Paper PMC6146921 Table 8 (experimental column) lists 1-heptanol with experimental BP 176.30 C.
Paper PMC12986465 reports 4-fluoro-5-(4-ethylpiperazin-1-yl)-2-nitroaniline (4c) as solid orange crystals with M.P. 143-145 C.

Paper PMC12986465 reports 6-fluoro-5-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazole (6g) with M.P. 190-192 C; (paper writes '4-pyrrolidin-1-yl' apparently a typographic '4-' but the structure/identity is the 5-pyrrolidin-1-yl analogue matching the row's compound name).
Paper PMC12984852 reports compound 1a (1-(4-methylbenzyl)-N-(piperidin-1-yl)-1,4-dihydroindeno[1,2-b]pirrole-3-carbohydrazide) as a beige solid with M.p. 152.5-154.6 C.
